Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Continued To Apr-21-25 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):

Martin Glickfeld, Esq., for Christian Muscat, Petitioner.
Elizabeth Erhardt, Esq., for Joseph Muscat, Respondent.

At the court's query, counsel presented updates regarding the position of the matter and whether the court should send this matter to Department 206 for trial.

At the conclusion of discussion, the subject matter is ordered continued to Department 206 for trial assignment on Apr-21-2025 at 9:30 a.m. Counsel is instructed to monitor the court's register of actions to obtain a copy of the order, if needed.

Judge: Hon. Ronald E. Quidachay. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Gomez. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial Setting Conference
Off Calendar Jan-28-2025 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Trial Setting Conference
Off Calendar Jan-07-2025 Continued To Jan-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jan-07-2025 Continued To Jan-28-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Continued To Jan-07-25 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Martin Glickfield, appearing via Zoom for Petitioner Christian Muscat.
Elizabeth Erhardt, appearing for Respondent Joseph James Muscat.

Ms. Erhardt explains her scheduling mishaps and extraordinary circumstances in her personal life that led to her failures to appear and to respond to the order to show cause. The court accepts Ms. Erhardt's explanation and orders the Order to Show Cause off calendar. The court is not ordering sanctions.

Discussions are held regarding a mediation that took place with Betty Epstein, which was unsuccessful. Mr. Glickfield informs the court that two depositions have been scheduled but have not yet been formally noticed, and expresses a willingness to mediate further after further discovery has been completed. Mr. Glickfield estimates 3 days of evidentiary hearings. The court explains that evidentiary hearings are limited to a maximum of 6 hours total, otherwise they are set for trial. Ms. Erhardt states that she believes 3 days is excessive and that everything can be accomplished within 6 hours.

The court continues the Petition to Determine the Validity of the February 25, 2019 Amendment, etc. to January 7, 2025, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 to allow the parties time to complete discovery. The court further sets a Trial Setting Conference for hearing on the same date and time and instructs counsel to file Case Management Statements no later than December 31, 2024 which is to include a witness list, the length of testimony and an exhibit list.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not reported (D204)

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Nov-19-2024 Off Calendar

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Sep-10-2024 Continued To Nov-19-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Glickfeld appeared by zoom for petitioner.

The court noted that Mrs. Erhardt is not present. Mr. Glickfeld stated that they had mediation with Mrs. Epstein, but it was unsuccessful, that he still needs to take several depositions and that he does not know why Mrs. Erhardt is not here. The Petition to Determine Validity of the February 25, 2019 Amendment and Restatement of the Separate Property Trust, etc. is continued to November 19, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Attorney Elizabeth Erhardt to appear on November 19, 2024, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why she failed to appear. An order to show cause re sanctions is also being issued for the same date for Mrs. Erhardt to appear and show cause as to why sanctions in the amount of $250.00 should not be imposed against her for failure to appear.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: N. Ng. Not reported.

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Jul-10-2024 Continued To Sep-10-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Glickfeld appeared in person for Christian Muscat.
Elizabeth Erhardt appeared by zoom for Joseph Muscat (respondent individually and as trustee).

Mr. Glickfeld stated that he has had cordial and productive conversation with counsel, that his client's deposition is scheduled for July 25th, and that the depositions of Mrs. Erhardt's client, a contingent beneficiary and the health care providers still need to be taken. Mr. Glickfeld also stated that they had mediation with Bette Epstein, but there weren't enough cards on the table, that once they complete the discovery process they can come back and attempt settlement again, and that he estimates a three-day trial. The Petition to Determine the Validity of the February 25, 2019 Amendment and Restatement, etc. is continued to September 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Pliego. Not reported.

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-01-2024 Continued To Jul-10-24 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Glickfeld appeared by zoom for petitioner.
Liz Erhardt appeared by zoom for Joseph Muscat (respondent).

Mr. Glickfeld stated that he filed a supplement to the petition to clear up the examiner's notes, that some depositions are needed and that he believes discovery can be completed in the next 60 days. Mrs. Erhardt stated that once she is able to get the deposition from the petitioner, she can get her end in order. Mr. Glickfeld stated that he will produce his client. The court encouraged counsel to work together to complete discovery and informed Mr. Glickfeld that proper notice needs to be provided to the contingent beneficiaries. The Petition to Determine Validity of Trust Amendment, etc. is continued to July 10, 2024 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar May-30-2023 Continued To Aug-15-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Glickfeld appeared by zoom for Christian Muscat.
Elizabeth Erhardt appeared by zoom for Joseph Muscat (respondent) individually and as a trustee of the trust.

The court noted that the parties want to go to mediation. Mrs. Erhardt confirmed that the sole asset of the trust is a 50% interest in J & J Muscat LLC and that its value is between 2 to 2.5 million. Mr. Glickfeld stated that he may need to do basic discovery and requested 90 days to complete mediation. The court noted that the contingent beneficiaries were not given notice and directed Mr. Glickfeld to take care of the notice issue. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than July 31, 2023. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by August 8, 2023. The Petition to Determine the Validity of the February 25, 2019 Amendment, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on August 15, 2023 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. The parties are to monitor the court's website to obtain a copy of the mediation order.

Judge: Stephen M. Murphy. Probate Examiner: Aeyoung Kim. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: Cristobal. Not reported.

Petition To Determine The Validity Of The February 25, 2019 Amendment And Restatement Of The Joseph Muscat Separate Property Trust Of 2005, For Accounting, And For Damages For Financial Elder Abuse Under The Welfare And Institution Code; For Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Nov-02-2022 Continued To Jan-11-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Martin Glickfeld, via Zoom, for Christian Muscat, Petitioner.

The court and counsel discuss the pending notice issue and how he plans to proceed. Counsel advises of serving litigation counsel via Notice and Acknowledgment of Receipt, pursuant to her agreement and of attempting to obtain a summons which was rejected by the clerk. Counsel is advised that this court has never seen a summons issued for probate matters. The court and counsel discuss whether venue is proper. The court advises that it believes that Petitioner has not established that venue is proper. After additional discussion, counsel recognizes that he may need to file a supplement or an amended petition and requests a continuance accordingly.

The matter is continued to Jan-11-2023 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Cristobal. Not Reported.
